raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should we target: (A) sentence-level polish and clarity only, preserving structure and claims; (B) polish plus section-level restructuring and clearer thesis/gap/contribution with claim–evidence tightening; or (C) B plus positioning novelty vs prior work and adding citations?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft would benefit from a clearer framing of the thesis, gap, and contribution to position the method within practical causal inference.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the problem-to-method-to-results-to-contribution chain is logically tight will make claims more defensible.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about bias reduction and MAE improvements should be explicitly aligned with the reported IHDP semi-synthetic results.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering the abstract-style components (motivation, classical approach, method, results, implications, summary) can strengthen the narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments are central to improving readability and professionalism.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should disclose uncertainties (e.g., semi-synthetic benchmarks, scope limits) to avoid overstating certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Language should avoid overbroad impact claims relative to the presented evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within the agreed improvement scope without adding new analyses beyond the draft.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to prevent wording that could mislead downstream policy decisions by implying unwarranted causal guarantees.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining clear linkage between claims and the stated datasets, baselines, and methods preserves traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should we target: (A) sentence-level polish and clarity only, preserving structure and claims; (B) polish plus section-level restructuring and clearer thesis/gap/contribution with claim–evidence tightening; or (C) B plus positioning novelty vs prior work and adding citations?

rationale.R1.1: The draft would benefit from a clearer framing of the thesis, gap, and contribution to position the method within practical causal inference.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the problem-to-method-to-results-to-contribution chain is logically tight will make claims more defensible.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about bias reduction and MAE improvements should be explicitly aligned with the reported IHDP semi-synthetic results.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering the abstract-style components (motivation, classical approach, method, results, implications, summary) can strengthen the narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments are central to improving readability and professionalism.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should disclose uncertainties (e.g., semi-synthetic benchmarks, scope limits) to avoid overstating certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Language should avoid overbroad impact claims relative to the presented evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within the agreed improvement scope without adding new analyses beyond the draft.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to prevent wording that could mislead downstream policy decisions by implying unwarranted causal guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining clear linkage between claims and the stated datasets, baselines, and methods preserves traceability.
